an9.36:1.1: “Relying on the first *jhāna*, *bhikkhus*, I speak of the destruction of the *āsavas*;
an9.36:1.2: relying on the second *jhāna*, *bhikkhus*, I speak of the destruction of the *āsavas*;
an9.36:1.3: relying on the third *jhāna*, *bhikkhus*, I speak of the destruction of the *āsavas*;
an9.36:1.4: relying on the fourth *jhāna*, *bhikkhus*, I speak of the destruction of the *āsavas*;
an9.36:1.5: relying on the dimension of infinite space [*ākāsānañcāyatanaṁ*], *bhikkhus*, I speak of the destruction of the *āsavas*;
an9.36:1.6: relying on the dimension of infinite *viññāṇa* [*viññāṇañcāyatanaṁ*], *bhikkhus*, I speak of the destruction of the *āsavas*;
an9.36:1.7: relying on the dimension of nothingness [*ākiñcaññāyatanaṁ*], *bhikkhus*, I speak of the destruction of the *āsavas*;
an9.36:1.8: relying on the dimension of neither-*saññā*-nor-non-*saññā* [*nevasaññānāsaññāyatanaṁ*], *bhikkhus*, I speak of the destruction of the *āsavas*;
an9.36:1.9: relying on the cessation of *saññā* and what is felt [*saññāvedayitanirodhaṁ*], *bhikkhus*, I speak of the destruction of the *āsavas*.
an9.36:10.1: Thus, *bhikkhus*, as far as there are attainments with *saññā*, so far there is penetration by final knowledge.
an9.36:10.2: And as for these two dimensions [*āyatanāni*], *bhikkhus*, relying on which—
an9.36:10.3: the attainment of the dimension of neither-*saññā*-nor-non-*saññā* [*nevasaññānāsaññāyatanasamāpatti*] and the cessation of *saññā* and what is felt [*saññāvedayitanirodho*]—these, *bhikkhus*, should be properly explained by meditators [*jhāyīhi*] skilled in attainments and skilled in emerging from attainments, after entering and emerging from them, I say.”
an9.36:2.1: ‘Relying on the first *jhāna*, *bhikkhus*, I speak of the destruction of the *āsavas*’: so it was said.
an9.36:2.2: In reference to what was this said?
an9.36:2.3: Here, *bhikkhus*, a *bhikkhu*, quite secluded from sensual pleasures … enters and dwells in the first *jhāna*.
an9.36:2.4: Whatever there is there connected with form [*rūpagataṁ*], connected with *vedanā*, connected with *saññā*, connected with *saṅkhāras*, connected with *viññāṇa*, he contemplates those *dhammas* as impermanent [*aniccato*], as *dukkha*, as a disease, as a boil, as a dart, as misery, as an affliction, as alien, as breaking apart, as empty, as not-self [*anattato*].
an9.36:2.5: He turns the mind [*cittaṁ*] away from those *dhammas*.
an9.36:2.6: Having turned the mind [*cittaṁ*] away from those *dhammas*, he directs the mind [*cittaṁ*] toward the deathless element:
an9.36:2.7: ‘This is peaceful, this is sublime: namely, the settling [*samatha*] of all *saṅkhāras*, the relinquishing of all attachments, the destruction of *taṇhā*, fading away, cessation, *nibbāna*.’
an9.36:2.8: Standing there, he reaches the destruction of the *āsavas*.
an9.36:2.9: If he does not reach the destruction of the *āsavas*, then by that very passion for the *dhamma*, by that delight in the *dhamma*, with the complete destruction of the five lower fetters, he becomes one reborn spontaneously, one who attains final *nibbāna* there, not liable to return from that world.
an9.36:3.1: Suppose, *bhikkhus*, an archer or an archer’s apprentice, having made a straw man or a clay mound into a target for practice, after some time becomes a long-distance shooter, a lightning-shot marksman, and a splitter of great masses;
an9.36:3.10: what was said thus was said in reference to this.
an9.36:3.2: so too, *bhikkhus*, a *bhikkhu*, quite secluded from sensual pleasures … enters and dwells in the first *jhāna*.
an9.36:3.3: Whatever there is there connected with form [*rūpagataṁ*], connected with *vedanā*, connected with *saññā*, connected with *saṅkhāras*, connected with *viññāṇa*, he contemplates those *dhammas* as impermanent [*aniccato*], as *dukkha*, as a disease, as a boil, as a dart, as misery, as an affliction, as alien, as breaking apart, as empty, as not-self [*anattato*].
an9.36:3.4: He turns the mind [*cittaṁ*] away from those *dhammas*.
an9.36:3.5: Having turned the mind [*cittaṁ*] away from those *dhammas*, he directs the mind [*cittaṁ*] toward the deathless element:
an9.36:3.6: ‘This is peaceful, this is sublime: namely, the settling [*samatha*] of all *saṅkhāras*, the relinquishing of all attachments, the destruction of *taṇhā*, fading away, cessation, *nibbāna*.’
an9.36:3.7: Standing there, he reaches the destruction of the *āsavas*.
an9.36:3.8: If he does not reach the destruction of the *āsavas*, then by that very passion for the *dhamma*, by that delight in the *dhamma*, with the complete destruction of the five lower fetters, he becomes one reborn spontaneously, one who attains final *nibbāna* there, not liable to return from that world.
an9.36:3.9: ‘Relying on the first *jhāna*, *bhikkhus*, I speak of the destruction of the *āsavas*’:
an9.36:4.10: Standing there, he reaches the destruction of the *āsavas*.
an9.36:4.11: If he does not reach the destruction of the *āsavas*, then by that very passion for the *dhamma*, by that delight in the *dhamma*, with the complete destruction of the five lower fetters, he becomes one reborn spontaneously, one who attains final *nibbāna* there, not liable to return from that world.
an9.36:4.3: ‘Relying on the fourth *jhāna*, *bhikkhus*, I speak of the destruction of the *āsavas*’: so it was said.
an9.36:4.4: In reference to what was this said?
an9.36:4.5: Here, *bhikkhus*, a *bhikkhu*, with the abandoning of *sukha* and the abandoning of *dukkha*, and with the previous disappearance of joy and sorrow, enters and dwells in the fourth *jhāna*, neither-*dukkha*-nor-*sukha*, with purity of *sati* through equanimity [*upekkhā*].
an9.36:4.6: Whatever there is there connected with form [*rūpagataṁ*], connected with *vedanā*, connected with *saññā*, connected with *saṅkhāras*, connected with *viññāṇa*, he contemplates those *dhammas* as impermanent [*aniccato*], as *dukkha*, as a disease, as a boil, as a dart, as misery, as an affliction, as alien, as breaking apart, as empty, as not-self [*anattato*].
an9.36:4.7: He turns the mind [*cittaṁ*] away from those *dhammas*.
an9.36:4.8: Having turned the mind [*cittaṁ*] away from those *dhammas*, he directs the mind [*cittaṁ*] toward the deathless element:
an9.36:4.9: ‘This is peaceful, this is sublime: namely, the settling [*samatha*] of all *saṅkhāras*, the relinquishing of all attachments, the destruction of *taṇhā*, fading away, cessation, *nibbāna*.’
an9.36:5.1: Suppose, *bhikkhus*, an archer or an archer’s apprentice, having made a straw man or a clay mound into a target for practice, after some time becomes a long-distance shooter, a lightning-shot marksman, and a splitter of great masses;
an9.36:5.2: so too, *bhikkhus*, a *bhikkhu*, with the abandoning of *sukha* … enters and dwells in the fourth *jhāna*.
an9.36:5.3: Whatever there is there connected with form [*rūpagataṁ*], connected with *vedanā* …
an9.36:5.4: not liable to return from that world.
an9.36:5.5: ‘Relying on the fourth *jhāna*, *bhikkhus*, I speak of the destruction of the *āsavas*’: what was said thus was said in reference to this.
an9.36:6.1: ‘Relying on the dimension of infinite space [*ākāsānañcāyatanaṁ*], *bhikkhus*, I speak of the destruction of the *āsavas*’: so it was said.
an9.36:6.2: In reference to what was this said?
an9.36:6.3: Here, *bhikkhus*, a *bhikkhu*, with the complete transcending of *saññās* of form [*rūpasaññānaṁ*], with the disappearance of *saññās* of resistance, with non-attention to *saññās* of diversity, thinking, ‘Space is infinite,’ enters and dwells in the dimension of infinite space [*ākāsānañcāyatanaṁ*].
an9.36:6.4: Whatever there is there connected with *vedanā*, connected with *saññā*, connected with *saṅkhāras*, connected with *viññāṇa*, he contemplates those *dhammas* as impermanent [*aniccato*], as *dukkha*, as a disease, as a boil, as a dart, as misery, as an affliction, as alien, as breaking apart, as empty, as not-self [*anattato*].
an9.36:6.5: He turns the mind [*cittaṁ*] away from those *dhammas*.
an9.36:6.6: Having turned the mind [*cittaṁ*] away from those *dhammas*, he directs the mind [*cittaṁ*] toward the deathless element:
an9.36:6.7: ‘This is peaceful, this is sublime: namely, the settling [*samatha*] of all *saṅkhāras*, the relinquishing of all attachments, the destruction of *taṇhā*, fading away, cessation, *nibbāna*.’
an9.36:6.8: Standing there, he reaches the destruction of the *āsavas*.
an9.36:6.9: If he does not reach the destruction of the *āsavas*, then by that very passion for the *dhamma*, by that delight in the *dhamma*, with the complete destruction of the five lower fetters, he becomes one reborn spontaneously, one who attains final *nibbāna* there, not liable to return from that world.
an9.36:7.1: Suppose, *bhikkhus*, an archer or an archer’s apprentice, having made a straw man or a clay mound into a target for practice, after some time becomes a long-distance shooter, a lightning-shot marksman, and a splitter of great masses;
an9.36:7.2: so too, *bhikkhus*, a *bhikkhu*, with the complete transcending of *saññās* of form [*rūpasaññānaṁ*], with the disappearance of *saññās* of resistance, with non-attention to *saññās* of diversity, thinking, ‘Space is infinite,’ enters and dwells in the dimension of infinite space [*ākāsānañcāyatanaṁ*].
an9.36:7.3: Whatever there is there connected with *vedanā*, connected with *saññā* …
an9.36:7.4: not liable to return from that world.
an9.36:7.5: ‘Relying on the dimension of infinite space [*ākāsānañcāyatanaṁ*], *bhikkhus*, I speak of the destruction of the *āsavas*’:
an9.36:7.6: what was said thus was said in reference to this.
an9.36:8.10: If he does not reach the destruction of the *āsavas*, then by that very passion for the *dhamma*, by that delight in the *dhamma*, with the complete destruction of the five lower fetters, he becomes one reborn spontaneously, one who attains final *nibbāna* there, not liable to return from that world.
an9.36:8.2: relying on the dimension of nothingness [*ākiñcaññāyatanaṁ*], *bhikkhus*, I speak of the destruction of the *āsavas*’: so it was said.
an9.36:8.3: In reference to what was this said?
an9.36:8.4: Here, *bhikkhus*, a *bhikkhu*, completely transcending the dimension of infinite *viññāṇa* [*viññāṇañcāyatanaṁ*], thinking, ‘There is nothing,’ enters and dwells in the dimension of nothingness [*ākiñcaññāyatanaṁ*].
an9.36:8.5: Whatever there is there connected with *vedanā*, connected with *saññā*, connected with *saṅkhāras*, connected with *viññāṇa*, he contemplates those *dhammas* as impermanent [*aniccato*], as *dukkha*, as a disease, as a boil, as a dart, as misery, as an affliction, as alien, as breaking apart, as empty, as not-self [*anattato*].
an9.36:8.6: He turns the mind [*cittaṁ*] away from those *dhammas*.
an9.36:8.7: Having turned the mind [*cittaṁ*] away from those *dhammas*, he directs the mind [*cittaṁ*] toward the deathless element:
an9.36:8.8: ‘This is peaceful, this is sublime: namely, the settling [*samatha*] of all *saṅkhāras*, the relinquishing of all attachments, the destruction of *taṇhā*, fading away, cessation, *nibbāna*.’
an9.36:8.9: Standing there, he reaches the destruction of the *āsavas*.
an9.36:9.1: Suppose, *bhikkhus*, an archer or an archer’s apprentice, having made a straw man or a clay mound into a target for practice, after some time becomes a long-distance shooter, a lightning-shot marksman, and a splitter of great masses;
an9.36:9.10: what was said thus was said in reference to this.
an9.36:9.2: so too, *bhikkhus*, a *bhikkhu*, completely transcending the dimension of infinite *viññāṇa* [*viññāṇañcāyatanaṁ*], thinking, ‘There is nothing,’ enters and dwells in the dimension of nothingness [*ākiñcaññāyatanaṁ*].

an9.36:9.5: Having turned the mind [*cittaṁ*] away from those *dhammas*, he directs the mind [*cittaṁ*] toward the deathless element:
an9.36:9.6: ‘This is peaceful, this is sublime: namely, the settling [*samatha*] of all *saṅkhāras*, the relinquishing of all attachments, the destruction of *taṇhā*, fading away, cessation, *nibbāna*.’
an9.36:9.7: Standing there, he reaches the destruction of the *āsavas*.
an9.36:9.8: If he does not reach the destruction of the *āsavas*, then by that very passion for the *dhamma*, by that delight in the *dhamma*, with the complete destruction of the five lower fetters, he becomes one reborn spontaneously, one who attains final *nibbāna* there, not liable to return from that world.
an9.36:9.9: ‘Relying on the dimension of nothingness [*ākiñcaññāyatanaṁ*], I speak of the destruction of the *āsavas*’:
